channel.title: Recent Articles in Phys. Rev. Lett.
channel.description: Recent articles in Physical Review Letters
channel.dc:creator: rss@aps.org
channel.dc:rights: Copyright © 2026 the American Physical Society. Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Nanoscale Imaging of Magnetotransport around a Circular $p\text{−}n$ Junction in Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Zachary J. Krebs, Wyatt A. Behn, Keenan J. Smith, Margaret A. Fortman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Pathak S. Parashar, Michael M. Fogler, and Victor W. Brar

Electrons in graphene follow various spiraling paths when they flow around a circular barrier under the influence of a magnetic field.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136301] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zachary J. Krebs, Wyatt A. Behn, Keenan J. Smith, Margaret A. Fortman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Pathak S. Parashar, Michael M. Fogler, and Victor W. Brar

Electrons in graphene follow various spiraling paths when they flow around a circular barrier under the influence of a magnetic field.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136301] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Nanoscale Imaging of Magnetotransport around a Circular $p\text{−}n$ Junction in Graphene
item.dc:creator: Zachary J. Krebs, Wyatt A. Behn, Keenan J. Smith, Margaret A. Fortman, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Pathak S. Parashar, Michael M. Fogler, and Victor W. Brar
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Electrically Driven Plasmon-Polaritonic Bistability in Dirac Electron Tunneling Transistors
item.description:
Author(s): Shuai Zhang, Yang Xu, Junhe Zhang, Dihao Sun, Yinan Dong, Matthew Fu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Cory Dean, Monica Allen, Jeffery Allen, F. Javier García de Abajo, Antti J. Moilanen, Lukas Novotny, and D. N. Basov

An electrically driven nonlinear mechanism based on resonant tunneling, plasmon-polaritonic bistability, unlocks the potential realization of polaritonic on-chip optical memory and switching.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136904] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuai Zhang, Yang Xu, Junhe Zhang, Dihao Sun, Yinan Dong, Matthew Fu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Cory Dean, Monica Allen, Jeffery Allen, F. Javier García de Abajo, Antti J. Moilanen, Lukas Novotny, and D. N. Basov

An electrically driven nonlinear mechanism based on resonant tunneling, plasmon-polaritonic bistability, unlocks the potential realization of polaritonic on-chip optical memory and switching.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136904] Published Thu Apr 02, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrically Driven Plasmon-Polaritonic Bistability in Dirac Electron Tunneling Transistors
item.dc:creator: Shuai Zhang, Yang Xu, Junhe Zhang, Dihao Sun, Yinan Dong, Matthew Fu, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Cory Dean, Monica Allen, Jeffery Allen, F. Javier García de Abajo, Antti J. Moilanen, Lukas Novotny, and D. N. Basov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: High Compression Blue-Detuned Magneto-Optical Trap of Polyatomic Molecules
item.description:
Author(s): Christian Hallas, Grace K. Li, Nathaniel B. Vilas, Paige Robichaud, Loïc Anderegg, and John M. Doyle

Researchers have improved trapping of polyatomic molecules while also controlling their collisions—two important advances for ultracold polyatomic molecular physics.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 133402] Published Wed Apr 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Christian Hallas, Grace K. Li, Nathaniel B. Vilas, Paige Robichaud, Loïc Anderegg, and John M. Doyle

Researchers have improved trapping of polyatomic molecules while also controlling their collisions—two important advances for ultracold polyatomic molecular physics.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 133402] Published Wed Apr 01, 2026
item.dc:title: High Compression Blue-Detuned Magneto-Optical Trap of Polyatomic Molecules
item.dc:creator: Christian Hallas, Grace K. Li, Nathaniel B. Vilas, Paige Robichaud, Loïc Anderegg, and John M. Doyle
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Quantum Critical Dynamics Induced by Topological Zero Modes
item.description:
Author(s): Ilia Komissarov, Tobias Holder, and Raquel Queiroz

We investigate low-frequency ac transport in the Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain with chiral disorder near the topological delocalization transition. Our key finding is that the formation of hybridized pairs of topological domain wall zero modes leads to the anomalous logarithmic scaling of the ac …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136602] Published Wed Apr 01, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ilia Komissarov, Tobias Holder, and Raquel Queiroz

We investigate low-frequency ac transport in the Su-Schrieffer-Heeger (SSH) chain with chiral disorder near the topological delocalization transition. Our key finding is that the formation of hybridized pairs of topological domain wall zero modes leads to the anomalous logarithmic scaling of the ac …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136602] Published Wed Apr 01, 2026
item.dc:title: Quantum Critical Dynamics Induced by Topological Zero Modes
item.dc:creator: Ilia Komissarov, Tobias Holder, and Raquel Queiroz
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Exact Duality at Low Energy in a Josephson Tunnel Junction Coupled to a Transmission Line
item.description:
Author(s): Luca Giacomelli, Michel H. Devoret, and Cristiano Ciuti

We theoretically explore the low-energy behavior of a Josephson tunnel junction coupled to a finite-length, charge-biased transmission line and compare it to its flux-biased counterpart. For transmission lines of increasing length, we show that the low-energy charge-dependent energy bands of the cha…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 130401] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Luca Giacomelli, Michel H. Devoret, and Cristiano Ciuti

We theoretically explore the low-energy behavior of a Josephson tunnel junction coupled to a finite-length, charge-biased transmission line and compare it to its flux-biased counterpart. For transmission lines of increasing length, we show that the low-energy charge-dependent energy bands of the cha…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 130401] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Exact Duality at Low Energy in a Josephson Tunnel Junction Coupled to a Transmission Line
item.dc:creator: Luca Giacomelli, Michel H. Devoret, and Cristiano Ciuti
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Gauge-Tunable Uniform Delocalization of Higher-Order Topological Photonic Modes
item.description:
Author(s): Shiqi Li, Yu He, Yunlang Wang, Shiyin Jia, Haotian Li, Renwen Huang, Hui Huang, Hongling Cai, Minghui Lu, Biye Xie, Peng Zhan, and Zhenlin Wang

Higher-order topological photonic systems typically host corner states that are exponentially localized. Here we uncover a distinct regime of uniformly delocalized higher-order topological modes, emerging from the interplay of multiple spatially varying Dirac mass terms under chiral symmetry. These …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 133801] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shiqi Li, Yu He, Yunlang Wang, Shiyin Jia, Haotian Li, Renwen Huang, Hui Huang, Hongling Cai, Minghui Lu, Biye Xie, Peng Zhan, and Zhenlin Wang

Higher-order topological photonic systems typically host corner states that are exponentially localized. Here we uncover a distinct regime of uniformly delocalized higher-order topological modes, emerging from the interplay of multiple spatially varying Dirac mass terms under chiral symmetry. These …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 133801] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Gauge-Tunable Uniform Delocalization of Higher-Order Topological Photonic Modes
item.dc:creator: Shiqi Li, Yu He, Yunlang Wang, Shiyin Jia, Haotian Li, Renwen Huang, Hui Huang, Hongling Cai, Minghui Lu, Biye Xie, Peng Zhan, and Zhenlin Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Angular Momentum Fluctuations in the Phonon Vacuum of Symmetric Crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Rule Yi, Violet Williams, and Benedetta Flebus

Although time-reversal and inversion symmetry constrain the angular momentum of each phonon mode to vanish, we show that the vacuum state of crystals with such symmetries can, nevertheless, exhibit finite angular momentum fluctuations, which persist at finite temperature. These fluctuations arise fr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136101] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rule Yi, Violet Williams, and Benedetta Flebus

Although time-reversal and inversion symmetry constrain the angular momentum of each phonon mode to vanish, we show that the vacuum state of crystals with such symmetries can, nevertheless, exhibit finite angular momentum fluctuations, which persist at finite temperature. These fluctuations arise fr…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136101] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Angular Momentum Fluctuations in the Phonon Vacuum of Symmetric Crystals
item.dc:creator: Rule Yi, Violet Williams, and Benedetta Flebus
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Soft Phonon Charge-Density-Wave Formation in the Kagome Metal ${\mathrm{KV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yifan Wang, Chenchao Xu, Zhimian Wu, Huachen Rao, Zhaoyang Shan, Yi Liu, Guanghan Cao, Michael Smidman, Ming Shi, Huiqiu Yuan, Tao Wu, Xianhui Chen, Chao Cao, and Yu Song

A range of unusual emergent behaviors have been reported in the charge-density-wave (CDW) state of the $A{\mathrm{V}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$ ($A=\mathrm{K}$, Rb, Cs) kagome metals, including a CDW formation process without soft phonons, which points to an unconventional CDW mechanism. Here, we use in…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136401] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yifan Wang, Chenchao Xu, Zhimian Wu, Huachen Rao, Zhaoyang Shan, Yi Liu, Guanghan Cao, Michael Smidman, Ming Shi, Huiqiu Yuan, Tao Wu, Xianhui Chen, Chao Cao, and Yu Song

A range of unusual emergent behaviors have been reported in the charge-density-wave (CDW) state of the AV3Sb5 (A=K, Rb, Cs) kagome metals, including a CDW formation process without soft phonons, which points to an unconventional CDW mechanism. Here, we use inelastic x-ray scattering to show that the…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136401] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Soft Phonon Charge-Density-Wave Formation in the Kagome Metal ${\mathrm{KV}}_{3}{\mathrm{Sb}}_{5}$
item.dc:creator: Yifan Wang, Chenchao Xu, Zhimian Wu, Huachen Rao, Zhaoyang Shan, Yi Liu, Guanghan Cao, Michael Smidman, Ming Shi, Huiqiu Yuan, Tao Wu, Xianhui Chen, Chao Cao, and Yu Song
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fractional Quantum Multiferroics from Coupling of Fractional Quantum Ferroelectricity and Altermagnetism
item.description:
Author(s): M. Q. Dong, B. Liu, Z. H. Dai, Zhi-Xin Guo, Hongjun Xiang, and Xin-Gao Gong

Introducing altermagnetism in fractional quantum ferroelectrics yields magnetoelectric coupling that can switch altermagnetic splitting without rotation of the Néel vector via fractional displacements.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136702] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): M. Q. Dong, B. Liu, Z. H. Dai, Zhi-Xin Guo, Hongjun Xiang, and Xin-Gao Gong

Introducing altermagnetism in fractional quantum ferroelectrics yields magnetoelectric coupling that can switch altermagnetic splitting without rotation of the Néel vector via fractional displacements.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136702] Published Tue Mar 31, 2026
item.dc:title: Fractional Quantum Multiferroics from Coupling of Fractional Quantum Ferroelectricity and Altermagnetism
item.dc:creator: M. Q. Dong, B. Liu, Z. H. Dai, Zhi-Xin Guo, Hongjun Xiang, and Xin-Gao Gong
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Superfluid Fraction of a 2D Bose-Einstein Condensate in a Triangular Lattice
item.description:
Author(s): F. Rabec, G. Brochier, S. Wattellier, G. Chauveau, Y. Li, S. Nascimbene, J. Dalibard, and J. Beugnon

The superfluid fraction of a 2D Bose-Einstein condensate is experimentally determined for the first time.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 133401] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): F. Rabec, G. Brochier, S. Wattellier, G. Chauveau, Y. Li, S. Nascimbene, J. Dalibard, and J. Beugnon

The superfluid fraction of a 2D Bose-Einstein condensate is experimentally determined for the first time.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 133401] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Superfluid Fraction of a 2D Bose-Einstein Condensate in a Triangular Lattice
item.dc:creator: F. Rabec, G. Brochier, S. Wattellier, G. Chauveau, Y. Li, S. Nascimbene, J. Dalibard, and J. Beugnon
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Emergence of a Fluctuating Ground State in Y-Kapellasite under Pressure
item.description:
Author(s): Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal

Hydrostatic pressure drives a magnetically ordered kagome material into a fluctuating ground state that shares key features with quantum spin liquids.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136701] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal

Hydrostatic pressure drives a magnetically ordered kagome material into a fluctuating ground state that shares key features with quantum spin liquids.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136701] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergence of a Fluctuating Ground State in Y-Kapellasite under Pressure
item.dc:creator: Dipranjan Chatterjee, Petr Doležal, Federico Abbruciati, Tobias Biesner, Katharina M. Zoch, Rustem Khasanov, Shams Sohel Islam, Guratinder Kaur, Seulki Roh, Francesco Capitani, Joao Elias F. S. Rodrigues, Gaston Garbarino, Cornelius Krellner, Philippe Mendels, Edwin Kermarrec, Martin Dressel, Björn Wehinger, Andrej Pustogow, Fabrice Bert, and Pascal Puphal
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Charge-Density Ripples Modulated by Nuclear Quantum Effects in High-Harmonic Generation in Solids
item.description:
Author(s): Shi-Qi Hu, Qing Chen, and Sheng Meng

Nuclear quantum effects (NQEs) significantly influence many fundamental physical and chemical phenomena. However, their impact on ultrafast dynamics remains poorly understood. Here, we demonstrate that solid-state high-harmonic generation (HHG) provides a promising platform for probing NQEs in attos…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136901] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shi-Qi Hu, Qing Chen, and Sheng Meng

Nuclear quantum effects (NQEs) significantly influence many fundamental physical and chemical phenomena. However, their impact on ultrafast dynamics remains poorly understood. Here, we demonstrate that solid-state high-harmonic generation (HHG) provides a promising platform for probing NQEs in attos…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 136901] Published Mon Mar 30, 2026
item.dc:title: Charge-Density Ripples Modulated by Nuclear Quantum Effects in High-Harmonic Generation in Solids
item.dc:creator: Shi-Qi Hu, Qing Chen, and Sheng Meng
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Antisymmetric Raman Response
item.description:
Author(s): Mattia Udina and Indranil Paul

We develop the theory of antisymmetric Raman response, defined as the difference between the Raman signals of two scattering geometries related by an exchange of mutually perpendicular incoming and the outgoing photon polarizations. Such responses, finite in orthorhombic or lower symmetry systems, a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126505] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Mattia Udina and Indranil Paul

We develop the theory of antisymmetric Raman response, defined as the difference between the Raman signals of two scattering geometries related by an exchange of mutually perpendicular incoming and the outgoing photon polarizations. Such responses, finite in orthorhombic or lower symmetry systems, a…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126505] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Antisymmetric Raman Response
item.dc:creator: Mattia Udina and Indranil Paul
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Element-Selective Probing of Ultrafast Ferromagnetic-Antiferromagnetic Order Dynamics in Fe/CoO Bilayers
item.description:
Author(s): Chowdhury S. Awsaf, Sangeeta Thakur, Markus Weißenhofer, Jendrik Gördes, Marcel Walter, Mohamad-Assaad Mawass, Niko Pontius, Christian Schüßler-Langeheine, Peter M. Oppeneer, and Wolfgang Kuch

The ultrafast magnetization dynamics of an epitaxial $\mathrm{Fe}/\mathrm{CoO}$ bilayer on Ag(001) is examined in an element-resolved way by resonant soft-x-ray reflectivity. The transient magnetic linear dichroism at the Co ${L}_{2}$ edge and the magnetic circular dichroism at the Fe ${L}_{3}$ edge…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126705] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chowdhury S. Awsaf, Sangeeta Thakur, Markus Weißenhofer, Jendrik Gördes, Marcel Walter, Mohamad-Assaad Mawass, Niko Pontius, Christian Schüßler-Langeheine, Peter M. Oppeneer, and Wolfgang Kuch

The ultrafast magnetization dynamics of an epitaxial Fe/CoO bilayer on Ag(001) is examined in an element-resolved way by resonant soft-x-ray reflectivity. The transient magnetic linear dichroism at the Co L2 edge and the magnetic circular dichroism at the Fe L3 edge measured in reflection in a pump–…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126705] Published Fri Mar 27, 2026
item.dc:title: Element-Selective Probing of Ultrafast Ferromagnetic-Antiferromagnetic Order Dynamics in Fe/CoO Bilayers
item.dc:creator: Chowdhury S. Awsaf, Sangeeta Thakur, Markus Weißenhofer, Jendrik Gördes, Marcel Walter, Mohamad-Assaad Mawass, Niko Pontius, Christian Schüßler-Langeheine, Peter M. Oppeneer, and Wolfgang Kuch
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Intrinsic Heralding and Optimal Decoders for Non-Abelian Topological Order
item.description:
Author(s): Dian Jing, Pablo Sala, Liang Jiang, and Ruben Verresen

Topological order (TO) provides a natural platform for storing and manipulating quantum information. However, its stability to noise has only been systematically understood for Abelian TOs. In this Letter, we exploit the nondeterministic fusion of non-Abelian anyons to inform active error-correction…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120405] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dian Jing, Pablo Sala, Liang Jiang, and Ruben Verresen

Topological order (TO) provides a natural platform for storing and manipulating quantum information. However, its stability to noise has only been systematically understood for Abelian TOs. In this Letter, we exploit the nondeterministic fusion of non-Abelian anyons to inform active error-correction…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120405] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Intrinsic Heralding and Optimal Decoders for Non-Abelian Topological Order
item.dc:creator: Dian Jing, Pablo Sala, Liang Jiang, and Ruben Verresen
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Quantum Information, Science, and Technology

item.title: Imaging Heat Transport in Suspended Diamond Nanostructures with Integrated Spin Defect Thermometers
item.description:
Author(s): V. Goblot, K. Wu, E. Di Lucente, Y. Zhu, E. Losero, Q. Jobert, C. Jaramillo Concha, N. Quack, N. Marzari, M. Simoncelli, and C. Galland

Among all materials, monocrystalline diamond has one of the highest measured thermal conductivities, with values above 2000 W/m/K at room temperature. Diamond nanostructures are increasingly used in photonics, electronics, and quantum technologies, where heat dissipation is critical. However, predic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126304] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): V. Goblot, K. Wu, E. Di Lucente, Y. Zhu, E. Losero, Q. Jobert, C. Jaramillo Concha, N. Quack, N. Marzari, M. Simoncelli, and C. Galland

Among all materials, monocrystalline diamond has one of the highest measured thermal conductivities, with values above 2000 W/m/K at room temperature. Diamond nanostructures are increasingly used in photonics, electronics, and quantum technologies, where heat dissipation is critical. However, predic…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126304] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Imaging Heat Transport in Suspended Diamond Nanostructures with Integrated Spin Defect Thermometers
item.dc:creator: V. Goblot, K. Wu, E. Di Lucente, Y. Zhu, E. Losero, Q. Jobert, C. Jaramillo Concha, N. Quack, N. Marzari, M. Simoncelli, and C. Galland
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Phase Space Fractons
item.description:
Author(s): Ylias Sadki, Abhishodh Prakash, S. L. Sondhi, and Daniel P. Arovas

Perhaps the simplest approach to constructing models with subdimensional particles or fractons is to require the conservation of dipole or higher multipole moments. We generalize this approach to allow for moments in phase space and classify all possible classical fracton models with phase-space mul…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126504] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ylias Sadki, Abhishodh Prakash, S. L. Sondhi, and Daniel P. Arovas

Perhaps the simplest approach to constructing models with subdimensional particles or fractons is to require the conservation of dipole or higher multipole moments. We generalize this approach to allow for moments in phase space and classify all possible classical fracton models with phase-space mul…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126504] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Phase Space Fractons
item.dc:creator: Ylias Sadki, Abhishodh Prakash, S. L. Sondhi, and Daniel P. Arovas
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Electrically Gated Laser-Induced Spin Dynamics in Magnetoelectric Iron Garnet at Room Temperature
item.description:
Author(s): T. T. Gareev, N. E. Khokhlov, L. Körber, A. P. Pyatakov, and A. V. Kimel

Ultrafast pump-probe imaging reveals that the efficiency of optical excitation of coherent spin waves in epitaxial iron garnet films can be effectively controlled by an external electric field at room temperature. Although a femtosecond laser pulse alone does not excite any pronounced coherent spin …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126702] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): T. T. Gareev, N. E. Khokhlov, L. Körber, A. P. Pyatakov, and A. V. Kimel

Ultrafast pump-probe imaging reveals that the efficiency of optical excitation of coherent spin waves in epitaxial iron garnet films can be effectively controlled by an external electric field at room temperature. Although a femtosecond laser pulse alone does not excite any pronounced coherent spin …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126702] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Electrically Gated Laser-Induced Spin Dynamics in Magnetoelectric Iron Garnet at Room Temperature
item.dc:creator: T. T. Gareev, N. E. Khokhlov, L. Körber, A. P. Pyatakov, and A. V. Kimel
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Light-Induced Odd-Parity Magnetism in Conventional Antiferromagnetism
item.description:
Author(s): Shengpu Huang, Zheng Qin, Fangyang Zhan, Dong-Hui Xu, Da-Shuai Ma, and Rui Wang

Odd-parity, f-wave or p-wave, spin splitting in 2D collinear antiferromagnets can be achieved through irradiation of light, and controlled by manipulating the polarization of the incident light and crystalline symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126703] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shengpu Huang, Zheng Qin, Fangyang Zhan, Dong-Hui Xu, Da-Shuai Ma, and Rui Wang

Odd-parity, f-wave or p-wave, spin splitting in 2D collinear antiferromagnets can be achieved through irradiation of light, and controlled by manipulating the polarization of the incident light and crystalline symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126703] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Light-Induced Odd-Parity Magnetism in Conventional Antiferromagnetism
item.dc:creator: Shengpu Huang, Zheng Qin, Fangyang Zhan, Dong-Hui Xu, Da-Shuai Ma, and Rui Wang
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Floquet Odd-Parity Collinear Magnets
item.description:
Author(s): Tongshuai Zhu, Di Zhou, Huaiqiang Wang, Su-Huai Wei, and Jiawei Ruan

Odd-parity, f-wave or p-wave, spin splitting in 2D collinear antiferromagnets can be achieved through irradiation of light, and controlled by manipulating the polarization of the incident light and crystalline symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126704] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Tongshuai Zhu, Di Zhou, Huaiqiang Wang, Su-Huai Wei, and Jiawei Ruan

Odd-parity, f-wave or p-wave, spin splitting in 2D collinear antiferromagnets can be achieved through irradiation of light, and controlled by manipulating the polarization of the incident light and crystalline symmetry.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126704] Published Thu Mar 26, 2026
item.dc:title: Floquet Odd-Parity Collinear Magnets
item.dc:creator: Tongshuai Zhu, Di Zhou, Huaiqiang Wang, Su-Huai Wei, and Jiawei Ruan
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Temperature-Dependent Single- and Double-Quantum Relaxation of Negatively Charged Boron Vacancies in Hexagonal Boron Nitride
item.description:
Author(s): Lin-Ke Xie, Wei Liu, Kaiyu Huang, Nai-Jie Guo, Jun-You Liu, Yu-Hang Ma, Ya-Qi Wu, Yi-Tao Wang, Zhao-An Wang, Xiao-Dong Zeng, Jia-Ming Ren, Chun Ao, Shuo Deng, Haifei Lu, Jian-Shun Tang, Chuan-Feng Li, and Guang-Can Guo

The negatively charged boron vacancy (${V}_{\text{B}}^{−}$) in two-dimensional (2D) hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) has emerged as a promising candidate for quantum sensing. The coherence time of ${V}_{\text{B}}^{−}$ spins which coherent quantum sensing resides in is limited by spin-phonon interaction…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 123603] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lin-Ke Xie, Wei Liu, Kaiyu Huang, Nai-Jie Guo, Jun-You Liu, Yu-Hang Ma, Ya-Qi Wu, Yi-Tao Wang, Zhao-An Wang, Xiao-Dong Zeng, Jia-Ming Ren, Chun Ao, Shuo Deng, Haifei Lu, Jian-Shun Tang, Chuan-Feng Li, and Guang-Can Guo

The negatively charged boron vacancy (VB−) in two-dimensional (2D) hexagonal boron nitride (hBN) has emerged as a promising candidate for quantum sensing. The coherence time of VB− spins which coherent quantum sensing resides in is limited by spin-phonon interactions, while the underlying physical m…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 123603] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Temperature-Dependent Single- and Double-Quantum Relaxation of Negatively Charged Boron Vacancies in Hexagonal Boron Nitride
item.dc:creator: Lin-Ke Xie, Wei Liu, Kaiyu Huang, Nai-Jie Guo, Jun-You Liu, Yu-Hang Ma, Ya-Qi Wu, Yi-Tao Wang, Zhao-An Wang, Xiao-Dong Zeng, Jia-Ming Ren, Chun Ao, Shuo Deng, Haifei Lu, Jian-Shun Tang, Chuan-Feng Li, and Guang-Can Guo
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Atomic, Molecular, and Optical Physics

item.title: Loop Current Order on the Kagome Lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Jun Zhan, Hendrik Hohmann, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ruiqing Fu, Sen Zhou, Ziqiang Wang, Ronny Thomale, Xianxin Wu, and Jiangping Hu

Recent discoveries in kagome materials have unveiled their capacity to harbor exotic quantum states, including intriguing charge density wave (CDW) and superconductivity. Notably, accumulating experimental evidence suggests time-reversal symmetry breaking within the CDW, hinting at the long-pursued …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126001] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jun Zhan, Hendrik Hohmann, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ruiqing Fu, Sen Zhou, Ziqiang Wang, Ronny Thomale, Xianxin Wu, and Jiangping Hu

Recent discoveries in kagome materials have unveiled their capacity to harbor exotic quantum states, including intriguing charge density wave (CDW) and superconductivity. Notably, accumulating experimental evidence suggests time-reversal symmetry breaking within the CDW, hinting at the long-pursued …


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126001] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Loop Current Order on the Kagome Lattice
item.dc:creator: Jun Zhan, Hendrik Hohmann, Matteo Dürrnagel, Ruiqing Fu, Sen Zhou, Ziqiang Wang, Ronny Thomale, Xianxin Wu, and Jiangping Hu

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Magnus-Driven Hall Transport in a Smectic Vortex State
item.description:
Author(s): Hiroyoshi Nobukane, Kyosuke Takahashi, Noriaki Matsunaga, Motoi Kimata, and Satoshi Tanda

We report the emergence of an anomalous Hall response induced by smectic vortex order in a layered superconductor. By tuning the in-plane magnetic field and temperature, we uncover a distinct transport regime in which the longitudinal resistance remains low, whereas the transverse resistance becomes…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126002] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hiroyoshi Nobukane, Kyosuke Takahashi, Noriaki Matsunaga, Motoi Kimata, and Satoshi Tanda

We report the emergence of an anomalous Hall response induced by smectic vortex order in a layered superconductor. By tuning the in-plane magnetic field and temperature, we uncover a distinct transport regime in which the longitudinal resistance remains low, whereas the transverse resistance becomes…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126002] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Magnus-Driven Hall Transport in a Smectic Vortex State

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Intertwined Swirling Polarization States in ${\mathrm{BaTiO}}_{3}$ with Embedded ${\mathrm{BaZrO}}_{3}$ Nanoregions
item.description:
Author(s): R. Machado, F. Di Rino, M. Sepliarsky, and M. G. Stachiotti

Ferroelectric materials embedded with dielectric inclusions offer a unique platform for exploring novel topological polar textures. Using first-principles-based atomistic simulations, we investigate the polarization behavior of a ${\mathrm{BaTiO}}_{3}$ matrix containing segregated ${\mathrm{BaZrO}}_…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126201] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): R. Machado, F. Di Rino, M. Sepliarsky, and M. G. Stachiotti

Ferroelectric materials embedded with dielectric inclusions offer a unique platform for exploring novel topological polar textures. Using first-principles-based atomistic simulations, we investigate the polarization behavior of a BaTiO3 matrix containing segregated BaZrO3 nanoregions. We demonstrate…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126201] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Intertwined Swirling Polarization States in ${\mathrm{BaTiO}}_{3}$ with Embedded ${\mathrm{BaZrO}}_{3}$ Nanoregions
item.dc:creator: R. Machado, F. Di Rino, M. Sepliarsky, and M. G. Stachiotti
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Fast Ionic Transport Governed by Collective Vibrational Dynamics
item.description:
Author(s): Yixin Xu, Xing Xiang, Zhigang Li, Yanglong Lu, and Yanguang Zhou

Here, we elucidate the role of collective vibrational dynamics in governing the ionic transport in solids. It is demonstrated that ionic transport is mediated through a synergistic interplay between unstable and stable vibrational modes. Unstable vibrational modes directly initiate the hopping of io…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126302] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yixin Xu, Xing Xiang, Zhigang Li, Yanglong Lu, and Yanguang Zhou

Here, we elucidate the role of collective vibrational dynamics in governing the ionic transport in solids. It is demonstrated that ionic transport is mediated through a synergistic interplay between unstable and stable vibrational modes. Unstable vibrational modes directly initiate the hopping of io…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126302] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Fast Ionic Transport Governed by Collective Vibrational Dynamics
item.dc:creator: Yixin Xu, Xing Xiang, Zhigang Li, Yanglong Lu, and Yanguang Zhou

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Orbital-Dependent Coulomb Drag in Electron-Hole Bilayer Graphene Heterostructures
item.description:
Author(s): Zuocheng Zhang, Ruishi Qi, Jingxu Xie, Qize Li, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Michael F. Crommie, and Feng Wang

Strong Coulomb drag is observed between Landau levels with nonzero orbital quantum numbers in a graphene heterostructure device consisting of an electron bilayer adjacent to a hole bilayer.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126303] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zuocheng Zhang, Ruishi Qi, Jingxu Xie, Qize Li, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Michael F. Crommie, and Feng Wang

Strong Coulomb drag is observed between Landau levels with nonzero orbital quantum numbers in a graphene heterostructure device consisting of an electron bilayer adjacent to a hole bilayer.


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126303] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Orbital-Dependent Coulomb Drag in Electron-Hole Bilayer Graphene Heterostructures
item.dc:creator: Zuocheng Zhang, Ruishi Qi, Jingxu Xie, Qize Li, Takashi Taniguchi, Kenji Watanabe, Michael F. Crommie, and Feng Wang

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Effective Enhancement of the Electron-Phonon Coupling Driven by Nonperturbative Electronic Density Fluctuations
item.description:
Author(s): E. Moghadas, M. Reitner, T. Wehling, G. Sangiovanni, S. Ciuchi, and A. Toschi

We present a dynamical mean-field study of nonperturbative electronic mechanisms, which may lead to significant enhancements of the electron-phonon coupling in correlated electron systems. Analyzing the effects of electronic correlations on the lowest-order electron-phonon processes, we show that in…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126502] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): E. Moghadas, M. Reitner, T. Wehling, G. Sangiovanni, S. Ciuchi, and A. Toschi

We present a dynamical mean-field study of nonperturbative electronic mechanisms, which may lead to significant enhancements of the electron-phonon coupling in correlated electron systems. Analyzing the effects of electronic correlations on the lowest-order electron-phonon processes, we show that in…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126502] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Effective Enhancement of the Electron-Phonon Coupling Driven by Nonperturbative Electronic Density Fluctuations

item.dc:subject: Condensed Matter and Materials

item.title: Phonon Induced Energy Relaxation in Quantum Critical Metals
item.description:
Author(s): Haoyu Guo and Debanjan Chowdhury

Metals at the brink of electronic quantum phase transitions display high-temperature superconductivity, competing orders, and unconventional charge transport, revealing strong departures from conventional Fermi liquid behavior. Investigation of these fascinating intertwined phenomena has been at the…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126503] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Haoyu Guo and Debanjan Chowdhury

Metals at the brink of electronic quantum phase transitions display high-temperature superconductivity, competing orders, and unconventional charge transport, revealing strong departures from conventional Fermi liquid behavior. Investigation of these fascinating intertwined phenomena has been at the…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126503] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026

item.title: Subthermal Mean Transverse Energies Induced by Electron Refraction on the Jump in Mass at the Surface of Multialkali Photocathodes
item.description:
Author(s): S. A. Rozhkov, V. V. Bakin, H. E. Scheibler, V. S. Rusetsky, D. V. Gorshkov, D. A. Kustov, V. A. Golyashov, V. L. Alperovich, and O. E. Tereshchenko

The search for photocathode materials with low mean transverse energies (MTEs) and, hence, low intrinsic emittance is of crucial importance for various fields of particle and solid-state physics. Here, we demonstrate that polycrystalline multialkali ${\mathrm{Na}}_{2}\mathrm{KSb}(\mathrm{Cs},\mathrm…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 127001] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): S. A. Rozhkov, V. V. Bakin, H. E. Scheibler, V. S. Rusetsky, D. V. Gorshkov, D. A. Kustov, V. A. Golyashov, V. L. Alperovich, and O. E. Tereshchenko

The search for photocathode materials with low mean transverse energies (MTEs) and, hence, low intrinsic emittance is of crucial importance for various fields of particle and solid-state physics. Here, we demonstrate that polycrystalline multialkali Na2KSb(Cs,Sb) photocathodes with negative effectiv…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 127001] Published Wed Mar 25, 2026
item.dc:title: Subthermal Mean Transverse Energies Induced by Electron Refraction on the Jump in Mass at the Surface of Multialkali Photocathodes

item.title: Fluctuation-Induced Giant Magnetoresistance in Charge-Neutral Graphene
item.description:
Author(s): A. Levchenko, E. Kirkinis, and A. V. Andreev

The Johnson-Nyquist noise associated with the intrinsic conductivity of the electron liquid induces fluctuations of the electron density in charge-neutral graphene devices. In the presence of external electric and magnetic fields, the fluctuations of charge density and electric current induce a fluc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126301] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. Levchenko, E. Kirkinis, and A. V. Andreev

The Johnson-Nyquist noise associated with the intrinsic conductivity of the electron liquid induces fluctuations of the electron density in charge-neutral graphene devices. In the presence of external electric and magnetic fields, the fluctuations of charge density and electric current induce a fluc…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126301] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Fluctuation-Induced Giant Magnetoresistance in Charge-Neutral Graphene

item.title: Biorthogonal Neural Network Approach to 2D Non-Hermitian Systems
item.description:
Author(s): Massimo Solinas, Brandon Barton, Yuxuan Zhang, Jannes Nys, and Juan Carrasquilla

Non-Hermitian (NH) quantum many-body systems exhibit a rich array of physical phenomena, including NH skin effects and exceptional points, that remain largely inaccessible to existing numerical techniques. In this Letter, we investigate the application of variational Monte Carlo and neural network w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126501] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Massimo Solinas, Brandon Barton, Yuxuan Zhang, Jannes Nys, and Juan Carrasquilla

Non-Hermitian (NH) quantum many-body systems exhibit a rich array of physical phenomena, including NH skin effects and exceptional points, that remain largely inaccessible to existing numerical techniques. In this Letter, we investigate the application of variational Monte Carlo and neural network w…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126501] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Biorthogonal Neural Network Approach to 2D Non-Hermitian Systems

item.title: Field-Free Electrical Switching of Perpendicular Magnetization via Domain-Wall-Free Textures in Ferrimagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Xueqiang Feng, Zhizhong Zhang, Yuze Xie, Zhenyi Zheng, Jinkai Wang, Zuojun Song, Bowen Yang, Kelian Lin, Guo Liu, Yu He, Bo Li, Weisheng Zhao, and Yue Zhang

Discovering and effectively leveraging nontrivial magnetic texture in magnetic materials is of great significance for developing next-generation spintronic devices. In this Letter, we demonstrate that solid-state hydrogen gating in rare-earth-transition-metal ferrimagnetic alloys can reversibly crea…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126701] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xueqiang Feng, Zhizhong Zhang, Yuze Xie, Zhenyi Zheng, Jinkai Wang, Zuojun Song, Bowen Yang, Kelian Lin, Guo Liu, Yu He, Bo Li, Weisheng Zhao, and Yue Zhang

Discovering and effectively leveraging nontrivial magnetic texture in magnetic materials is of great significance for developing next-generation spintronic devices. In this Letter, we demonstrate that solid-state hydrogen gating in rare-earth-transition-metal ferrimagnetic alloys can reversibly crea…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 126701] Published Tue Mar 24, 2026
item.dc:title: Field-Free Electrical Switching of Perpendicular Magnetization via Domain-Wall-Free Textures in Ferrimagnets
item.dc:creator: Xueqiang Feng, Zhizhong Zhang, Yuze Xie, Zhenyi Zheng, Jinkai Wang, Zuojun Song, Bowen Yang, Kelian Lin, Guo Liu, Yu He, Bo Li, Weisheng Zhao, and Yue Zhang

item.title: Engineering Long-Range and Multibody Interactions via Global Kinetic Constraints
item.description:
Author(s): Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao

Long-range and multibody interactions are crucial for quantum simulation and quantum computation. Yet, their practical realization using elementary pairwise interactions remains an outstanding challenge. We propose an experimental scheme based on the Bose-Hubbard system with a periodic driving of th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120401] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao

Long-range and multibody interactions are crucial for quantum simulation and quantum computation. Yet, their practical realization using elementary pairwise interactions remains an outstanding challenge. We propose an experimental scheme based on the Bose-Hubbard system with a periodic driving of th…


[Phys. Rev. Lett. 136, 120401] Published Mon Mar 23, 2026
item.dc:title: Engineering Long-Range and Multibody Interactions via Global Kinetic Constraints
item.dc:creator: Runmin Wu, Bing Yang, Pieter W. Claeys, and Hongzheng Zhao
